# criblio_search_dataset_provider (Resource)

SearchDatasetProvider Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_search_dataset_provider" "my_searchdatasetprovider" {
  prometheus = {
    auth_type       = "basic"
    description     = "my generic provider description"
    endpoint        = "https://prometheus.example.com"
    id              = "myUniqueGenericProviderId"
    max_concurrency = 10
    password        = "prom_pass"
    token           = "prometheusBearerToken123"
    type            = "generic"
    username        = "prom_user"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->

<a id="nestedatt--snowflake"></a>
### Nested Schema for `snowflake`

Optional:

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the provider
- `type` (String)
- `description` (String) Human-readable description of the provider
- `account_identifier` (String) The Snowflake account identifier, in the format <orgname>-<account_name>
- `username` (String) The Snowflake user for key pair authentication
- `priv_key` (String) The private key string out of the key file, from the pair of keys generated for authentication
- `passphrase` (String, Sensitive) The key password for the private key
- `max_concurrency` (Integer) Total number of concurrent executors for processing Snowflake partitions
- `endpoint` (String) Optional endpoint to override default REST API endpoint

<a id="nestedatt--clickhouse"></a>
### Nested Schema for `clickhouse`

Optional:

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the provider
- `type` (String)

- `username` (String) The ClickHouse username for authentication
- `password` (String, Sensitive) The ClickHouse user password for authentication
- `endpoint` (String) URL to ClickHouse server with HTTP interface enabled. Ideally should be HTTPS over port 8443.

<a id="nestedatt--api_aws--account_configs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `api_aws.account_configs`

Optional:

- `assume_role_arn` (String) IAM role ARN to assume when using automatic credentials.
- `assume_role_external_id` (String) External ID required by the role trust policy when assuming the role.
- `aws_api_key` (String) AWS access key ID when using manual key authentication.
- `aws_secret_key` (String, Sensitive) AWS secret access key when using manual key authentication.
- `aws_session_token` (String) Temporary session token for short-lived credentials.
- `name` (String) Display name that identifies the account configuration.

<a id="nestedatt--api_azure--account_configs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `api_azure.account_configs`

Optional:

- `client_id` (String) Azure AD application (client) ID for API access.
- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) Azure AD application client secret.
- `name` (String) Display name that identifies the account configuration.
- `tenant_id` (String) Azure AD tenant ID that owns the application registration.

<a id="nestedatt--api_gcp--account_configs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `api_gcp.account_configs`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Display name that identifies the account configuration.
- `service_account_credentials` (String) JSON key downloaded from Google Cloud Console for a service account that can call GCP APIs.

<a id="nestedatt--api_google_workspace--account_configs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `api_google_workspace.account_configs`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Display name that identifies the account configuration.
- `service_account_credentials` (String) JSON key material for a Google Workspace domain-wide delegated service account.
- `subject` (String) Admin user email address that the service account impersonates for Reports API access.

<a id="nestedatt--api_msgraph--account_configs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `api_msgraph.account_configs`

Optional:

- `client_id` (String) Application (client) ID registered in Entra ID.
- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) Client secret for the registered application.
- `name` (String) Display name that identifies the account configuration.
- `tenant_id` (String) Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) tenant ID.

<a id="nestedatt--api_okta--account_configs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `api_okta.account_configs`

Optional:

- `api_token` (String) API token used to authorize Okta API requests.

- `name` (String) Display name that identifies the account configuration.

<a id="nestedatt--api_tailscale--account_configs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `api_tailscale.account_configs`

Optional:

- `client_id` (String) OAuth client ID from the Tailscale API credentials.

- `name` (String) Display name that identifies the account configuration.

<a id="nestedatt--api_zoom--account_configs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `api_zoom.account_configs`

Optional:

- `account_id` (String) Zoom account identifier tied to these credentials.

- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) OAuth client secret from the Zoom Marketplace app.
- `name` (String) Display name that identifies the account configuration.

<a id="nestedatt--azure_blob--sas_configs"></a>
### Nested Schema for `azure_blob.sas_configs`

Optional:

- `container_name` (String) Name of the Azure Blob Storage container
- `blob_sas_url` (String) Container-specific Blob SAS URL

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_search_dataset_provider.my_criblio_search_dataset_provider
  id = "provider-s3-main"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_search_dataset_provider.my_criblio_search_dataset_provider "provider-s3-main"
```
